title: Chapter Seventeen: Return
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He was at the end of the torchlit passage.

Different from the others. Hair not fully red -- streaks of black growing back through the crimson, growing in at the roots. It was said that the red hair of Blood Flame Cult martial artists revealed the refinement of their inner energy, and that some of their body hair gradually returning to its original state meant they had reached a level of purifying their vital energy.

The ominous energy pouring off him was a physical thing, pressing against Yeon-shin's skin like heat from an open furnace. It was palpable even at ten paces -- sinister, layered, the signature of a man who had killed and consumed for years.

Blood Master Sword.

His appearance was exactly as rumoured. Branch leaders of the Blood Flame Cult were genuine masters. The sword that teaches and guides.

*This won't be easy.*

A boy of fifteen single-handedly taking on a branch of the Blood Flame Cult should have been unimaginable. And yet here he stood, with eight dead cultists behind him and the branch leader ahead. The same confidence that had carried him this far must also be in the mind of the man slowly approaching with his sword drawn. Even after killing several cult members, this was different. The Blood Master Sword, with his growing black hair, was on another level entirely from the pure-red minions.

"You worthless thing." The Blood Master Sword came forward, his face a mask of rage, his aura shooting outward as if it were made of hundreds of blades. "You've ruined our sect's affairs. I'll replenish my blood with your pain."

"You mean to steal vital energy through blood techniques?"

Not a question born of curiosity. He had been gauging the situation before blurting it out. A measure of distance. Two more steps.

*Here I go.*

His dantian opened wide. The vital energy of the Jeong family's Dynamic Technique rushed through his entire body, filling every meridian while embodying the Fate Conquering Scripture. The speed of mobilising inner energy was different from when he had been at the Jeong family manor. Everything about him was different now.

*I need to overwhelm him before he gets close.*

He had realised the effectiveness of striking first by defeating the Blue-rank expert of the Azure Sky Squad. Seize the initiative. Do not give them time to settle.

He made a large half-turn, stepping in the footwork of the unnamed leaf. Vital energy concentrated with strength in his waist and thighs. The air was threateningly torn as he unleashed a back kick with the full force of the Radiant Demon Formation behind it.

A look of surprise flashed across the Blood Master Sword's face as Yeon-shin came at him with his back to the torches, but the man avoided the kick by leaning back his chest. A finger's width of clearance.

"Hup!"

The counter-thrust came instantly -- a veteran's reaction, an angry thrust aimed at the opening Yeon-shin had created. But that was precisely where the trap lay.

Yeon-shin kicked off the ground with his right foot, which had swiftly descended through the air. He sharply rushed in, body tilted low, cutting off the timing of the sword strike in an instant. A gust of wind accompanied his body movement as he rapidly closed in on the careless enemy.

His left hand stretched out amidst the self-generated divine wind, aiming for the opponent's wrist. The Grappling Hand -- the first technique he had unleashed at the Jeong family manor before its destruction, guided by instinct from the Dynamic Technique. The Radiant Demon Formation's momentum was added to the grab.

Yeon-shin knew how to use his appearance and age like the fangs of a beast. Once he bit down, it was over.

His fingers locked around the Blood Master Sword's wrist. The monstrous strength of the Dynamic Technique was imbued in his grip as he latched on and seized the pulse point.

"What--!"

"Aaaagh!"

Sinister energy erupted above and below. Knee driving upward. Knife-hand chopping down. Yeon-shin did not need to look. He felt the trajectories in the air.

The Desolate Sword, reversed in his grip, came first.

*The Radiant Demon Formation does not discriminate between techniques.*

Ma Jin's voice. A month ago, in the training hall. A hint of laughter in it, even then.

He thrust the reversed blade inward. Not a sword path. Raw power. The Charged Rotational Strike overlapping in his right arm, building, compounding. The mythic Pangu pushing back a mountain.

Deep. The sinister energy scattered into nothing.

"It seems you've only inflicted pain on others," Yeon-shin said quietly. "I can tell. You've never experienced it yourself."

The screams choked off as he drove the blade deeper. He held the dying man close enough to feel the last heartbeats fade.

It did not move him. What had hardened through his family's destruction was proof against this.

"There's a saying in the martial world. Beware the elderly, women, and children."

The Blood Master Sword did not hear. He was already dead.

Yeon-shin pushed the body away and stood alone.

Around him, children lay crumpled against the cave walls. Emaciated. Hollow-eyed. Some dead, some barely breathing. Boys and girls, none older than ten.

*The Jeong family manor. The bodies in the courtyard.*

The door rattled.

He kept it shut.

He crouched beside each child and closed their eyes.

"Go to the paradise of Peach Blossom Spring," he whispered.

He drew his knife, cut the Blood Master Sword's head free, and wrapped it in a strip of the dead man's robe. Found the last surviving child -- ribs showing through skin like paper -- and lifted the boy onto his back.

Then he walked down the mountain.

---

"You've broken your blade. What happened? Are you alright?"

Baek Mi-ryeo's voice. Concern in it -- the tone of an older sister, which he had never had.

He came down with blood-soaked robes that were not his own and a child on his back.

"It's not my blood. I found a branch of the Blood Flame Cult."

"A branch!" Her eyes sharpened. "How many did you engage?"

"Eight with red hair. One with mixed black and red."

"Blood Master Sword...!"

Cheong Myeong dropped from above, landing with the scent of grass and a rare intensity in his face.

"You turned an entire Blood Flame Cult branch into a wasteland by yourself?"

"They had already turned it into a bloody wasteland."

Their eyes went to the child on his back. Baek Mi-ryeo's jaw clenched. "Damn cult bastards."

"Those Blood Master Sword types have regenerative abilities," Cheong Myeong said. "You didn't get stabbed in the back?"

"We should have told you sooner," Baek Mi-ryeo added, her expression unusually apologetic. "Even in Desolate City, we hear stories. Masters who defeated a Blood Master Sword, then died because they didn't know about the blood techniques."

Yeon-shin listened. Then he reached behind his back and held out the severed head.

Red and black hair. Wide-open eyes on a pale face. Unclosed.

Silence.

"Regenerative ability," Yeon-shin said. "So that's what it was."

Cheong Myeong stared at the head for a long moment.

"Right," the Elf said, his expression disgruntled. "Regenerative ability. That does exist."

Baek Mi-ryeo patted his shoulder. "We must acknowledge your merit. That's an excellent attitude."

Behind them, Heon Won-chang turned the colour of chalk.

"It's dead," Cheong Myeong said. "What are you afraid of?"

"I'm not scared! Do you know how many bandits have met their end by these hands!"

The group laughed. It felt like relief.

---

The subsequent events were out of Radiant Demon Wing's hands.

Four days to wrap up the incident. The magistrate, shocked white upon seeing the severed head of the Blood Master Sword, sent garrison troops to the cave. There were stories that some of the soldiers wet themselves upon seeing the Blood Flame Cult's branch turned into carnage. The notoriety of the Blood Master Sword was even more famous among civilians, separate from their actual martial prowess.

The corpses strewn about the branch were not children from Zhenping County. It was presumed that the Blood Flame Cult members had captured them from nearby areas, but since the disappearances were never connected to martial artists, no news had reached Desolate City.

When the magistrate learned that terrifying blood demons had been secretly operating in his county, he grabbed Yeon-shin's hands with both of his own and repeatedly expressed his gratitude, as if he had never been hesitant before.

On the morning of departure, Yu Geun stood at the road, looking up at the mounted group. The scene was completely different from their first encounter. His gaze toward Yeon-shin burned with something that was not purely respect.

"If you have occasion to visit again, would you please stop by once more?"

*He wants to make connections with the martial arts expert he's only heard about, and even more so with a promising young talent.* Cheong Myeong's telepathic message was tinged with amusement.

Though Yeon-shin had not experienced much of the outside world, he could sense what was in the magistrate's eyes. Calculation beneath the gratitude. The ambition of a provincial official who had discovered the value of knowing a rising martial artist.

*Yu Geun of Zhenping County. Perhaps a person of greater capacity than he appears. It is admirable that he can act this way despite the humiliation of having feared us for a month.*

"I am Yu Geun of Zhenping County! Please be sure to remember!"

The group turned their horses and left. The incident, which had been long yet short, peaceful yet cruelly concluded, was finally settled. The first venture into the martial world for the two White-ranks of Radiant Demon Wing ended with a record of Lightning Flash's solo achievement.

---

The return journey was more leisurely than the ride out. They took long meal breaks for the horses and stayed at inns rather than camping. Thanks to the gentler pace, Heon Won-chang's face regained its colour, though he often wore a pitiful expression.

"Young Master Jeong has accomplished a brilliantly unparalleled feat, while I have done nothing..."

"That's actually more normal," Baek Mi-ryeo said the first time. "Just keeping watch and preventing the atrocities of the cult member is enough."

After the third repetition, she rode in silence.

Rather than camping out, they stayed at roadside inns and slowly entered Xiangyang. The first thing that caught Yeon-shin's eye was the fortress of Desolate City, even larger than Xiangyang's outer walls.

He smiled slightly.

*Home.*

The word surprised him. He had not been there long enough for it to feel that way. Strange to feel a welcoming familiarity despite such a short stay. But there it was.

"The atmosphere seems somewhat excited," Cheong Myeong said, closing his eyes on horseback. His leaf-shaped ears twitched, catching conversations from a hundred paces away. The group fell silent -- they were accustomed to the keen hearing characteristic of the Elf clans. It was thanks to Cheong Myeong that none of them had stood night watch for the entire journey.

"It seems that person is coming," the Elf said. "This will be a windfall for everyone here, considering how Desolate City greatly honours those who have achieved merits."

"I just heard it too," Baek Mi-ryeo said. "The former leader of the Divine Sword Squad is finally..."

Yeon-shin's body stiffened.

She glanced at him as if finding it strange, then continued. "If he's challenging for the position of Elder Council Head, he wouldn't pass up the public sentiment in Xiangyang."

"The head of Desolate City's Elder Council would be closer to the imperial family than even Xiangyang's branch leader," Cheong Myeong said. "This street -- I think each person might get at least a measure of rice. How excited they must be."

"He passed by with a boy riding in front of him on the horse," Baek Mi-ryeo added. "That child is the adopted son of the Desolate Ma family. I've heard his qualities are extraordinary."

*Desolate Ma family.*

His mother's people. The family that had severed ties with a daughter who married beneath them. Who had not come to the wedding, nor to the funeral.

Cheong Myeong urged his horse forward, uninterested. "Qualities, huh."

Baek Mi-ryeo's jet-black hair swayed with the horse's steps as she smirked. She turned her head slightly and glanced at Yeon-shin.

He nodded. His face was calm.

They stroked their horses' necks and matched pace with the group, and the four riders of Radiant Demon Wing arrived at Desolate City's main gate.

But something had arrived before them, and the weight of it sat against the back of Yeon-shin's neck like a hand he did not want to acknowledge.

---
